executive_summary.savage_verdict: Your commands have a PhD in overthinking and a master's degree in complexity addiction
key_findings: Readability crisis: 89% chance junior dev runs screaming

savage_analysis.description: A 818-line command that preaches simplicity while violating every rule it sets
contradictions: Demands functions < 20 lines, but is 818 lines long itself
Preaches 'simplicity first' while being complexity incarnate
Says 'can you explain in ONE sentence?' - answer: NO, takes 818 lines
actual_problems: Would take 45+ minutes to read, let alone use
Violates its own complexity score rule (4.8 > 5)
Junior developers would need therapy after reading this

savage_analysis.description: Routing system to manage complexity that is itself... complex
actual_problems: 500 lines to decide if something is complex
Has 58 thinking blocks to think about thinking
Meta-complexity managing complexity - infinite recursion much?
Like hiring a committee to decide if you need a committee

savage_analysis.description: Adds reasoning to commands... by making them unreasonably complex
actual_problems: 414 lines to add 'reasoning' to simple tasks
Would take longer to read than to just think
Meta-wrapper that needs its own wrapper
Reasoning about reasoning about reasoning - turtles all the way down

savage_analysis.description: ADHD-friendly command that requires 20+ minute attention span to understand
actual_problems: 400 lines is the opposite of ADHD-friendly
Would cause context switch fatigue while preventing it
More complex than the problem it solves
Person with ADHD would abandon this command halfway through reading

savage_analysis.description: Most reasonable of the bunch, but still overthinks thinking
actual_problems: 356 lines to think deeply - most people can just... think
Interactive questions that aren't actually interactive
redemption_notes: Actually focused on solving real problems
Reasonable complexity for what it does

success_probability.interpretation: 60% chance of user giving up before completion

what_doesnt: More complex than problems they solve
brutal_but_fair_assessment.the_naked_truth: You've created a system that fights complexity by being complex. It's like fighting fire with gasoline - technically impressive, practically questionable.

immediate_actions: Limit thinking blocks to 5 per command
Apply own simplicity rules to commands themselves
Create 'simple mode' versions of each command
structural_changes: Add TL;DR sections for each command

scientific_methodology.measurement_approach: Objective metrics with subjective reality testing
bias_controls: Measured against stated goals in CLAUDE.md
scientific_methodology.confidence_assessment: High confidence in relative rankings, moderate confidence in absolute values

final_savage_verdict.justification: Commands work but violate their own principles. Like a diet book written by someone who's never seen a vegetable.
final_savage_verdict.quote_for_posterity: Your commands have achieved the remarkable feat of being both overly complex and insufficiently useful - a true engineering antihero story.
final_savage_verdict.path_to_redemption: Embrace the simplicity you preach. Your future self (and every developer who has to use these) will thank you.
